summary: Frontend-only E2E of ForgeSlicer iter-149.3 Release C (Menu reorg + Box Designer). 10/10 tests pass after applying a 1-line fix to BoxDesignerDialog.jsx for the ZIP-bundle download (JSZip does not accept DataView — converted to Uint8Array).

ui_bugs.issue: handleDownloadZip passed a DataView (return type of geometryToSTLBinary) directly to JSZip.file(). JSZip 3 supports only string/Blob/ArrayBuffer/Uint8Array/base64 — DataView is unsupported and causes generateAsync() to hang forever. Result: 'ZIP BUNDLE' click had no effect, no download, no toast, no console error (silent failure).
ui_bugs.fix_applied: Convert DataView -> new Uint8Array(dv.buffer, dv.byteOffset, dv.byteLength) before zip.file(). Individual Box / Lid downloads already used new Blob([dv],...) which supports DataView, so they were not affected.

ui_bugs.issue: 'What's New' Sonner toast (v1.1 update) covers the bottom-right corner where the Box Designer's ZIP BUNDLE button lives. force=True bypasses hit-testing so functional tests still work, but a real user might miss/misclick the button. Suggest either dismissing the toast on dialog open or shifting the toast when a modal is present.

action_items: Fix applied: BoxDesignerDialog.jsx ZIP export DataView → Uint8Array. Please review the 1-line change.
Optional polish: auto-dismiss the 'What's New' Sonner toast when a modal (e.g. Box Designer) opens, since it can overlap footer action buttons in the 1200×760 dialog.
critical_code_review_comments: BoxDesignerDialog.jsx: geometryToSTLBinary() returns a DataView; JSZip.file() silently mishandles DataView. Bug fixed.
boxGenerator.js: unused-var placeholder scoopH is dead code (only side-effect: void scoopH). Consider removing or actually implementing oval scoops for handles.
BoxDesignerDialog is 450+ lines but focused/self-contained; still within acceptable size. Consider extracting NumField/CheckField into a small shared components module if reused elsewhere.

The `hinged` and `sliding` lid modes cause 'Lid' bbox to grow (60×43.75×8.3 for hinged, 60×40×3.35 for sliding). This is correct behaviour (tabs/rails extend past base lid slab) but the review_request expected sliding lid == 'gets rails' without specifying bbox growth — behaviour matches spec.
Test-request Test 5 line 'friction' expected z ≈ 5mm — actual z is exactly 5.0mm. Verified.
test_credentials: session_token=st_admin_health_1784561381387 (cookie on preview host) — worked for /workspace access.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Release C features (menu reorg + Box Designer) are fully functional after the ZIP DataView fix. Verified: tabs [3D,2D,COMBO,PARAM,AI] in order with no LIB tab; COMBO holds Composites + Component Library grid with all filter chips; PARAM shows Box Designer + Drawer Chest 'soon' placeholder; Box Designer opens, live preview builds a 60×40×28 body + 2mm drop lid; all 5 lid modes (none/drop/sliding/hinged/friction) build without error; friction lid grows to 5mm (skirt) as expected; compartments 3×2 rebuilds cleanly; stackable/side-handles/label-recess toggles all rebuild; label-recess reveals depth field; Box.stl / Lid.stl / ZIP downloads all succeed (ZIP contains 2 STLs + README.txt with parameter dump); Add-to-workspace closes the dialog and adds 2 imported meshes named 'Box body' and 'Lid'; regression on 3D/2D/AI tabs + STL export via toolbar still works.

test_results_matrix.test2_combo_merged: PASS (structural — all required data-testids present: library-cat-filter, library-grid, add-slot-negative-btn, add-slot-positive-btn, add-fastener-pair-btn, open-hardware-library-btn, open-texture-library-btn, add-hole-dialog-btn, add-countersink-btn, add-hex-pocket-btn, add-gusset-btn)
test_results_matrix.test4_designer_opens: PASS (bbox: Box 60×40×28 mm, Lid 60×40×2 mm; showlid toggle works)
test_results_matrix.test5_lid_modes: PASS (none→no lid; drop→z=2; sliding→z=3.35; hinged→z=8.3 (extended by knuckle+tabs, y grows to 43.75); friction→z=5)
test_results_matrix.test6_compartments: PASS (3×2 rebuilds, bbox stays 60×40×28, no errors)
test_results_matrix.test7_extras: PASS (all three toggles rebuild without error; label-depth field appears when label recess is on)
test_results_matrix.test8_downloads: PASS after fix (Box: Untitled_Project_box.stl 18884B, Lid: 7084B, ZIP: 22113B with 2×STL + README.txt)
test_results_matrix.test9_add_workspace: PASS (dialog closes, scene-count 0→2, outliner shows 'Box body' + 'Lid')
test_results_matrix.test10_regression: PASS (Rect Solid + Sphere + Triangle spawn correctly; export-stl-btn downloads 108284B .stl)

rca_of_the_zip_bug.root_cause: geometryToSTLBinary (exporters.js line 47-50) returns the value from STLExporter.parse(mesh, {binary: true}), which is a DataView. In handleDownloadZip we passed this DataView directly to JSZip.file(name, data). JSZip 3.x's compressed-content pipeline does not include DataView in its dispatch table; the input hits the string-coercion fallback but subsequent CRC32/deflate path stalls because the promised-content resolver hangs on the non-matching type. Result: generateAsync({type:'blob'}) never resolves or rejects — no toast, no download, no console error.
rca_of_the_zip_bug.reproduction: Open workspace → PARAM tab → Box Designer → click 'ZIP BUNDLE'. Before fix: nothing happens (button stays enabled, no spinner, no toast). Individual Box/Lid downloads work because they use new Blob([dv],{type:'model/stl'}) which does accept DataView.
rca_of_the_zip_bug.mitigation_applied: 1-line change: const bytes = new Uint8Array(dv.buffer, dv.byteOffset, dv.byteLength); zip.file(name, bytes). Uint8Array is JSZip's happy path.
rca_of_the_zip_bug.verification: Post-fix expect_download() resolves in <2s; ZIP is 22113B; contains Untitled_Project_box.stl, Untitled_Project_lid.stl, README.txt with full parameter dump.
